Small Talk For 2-3 Minutes

Set The Frame


The Script:


So this is how my discovery meetings usually go. I donï¿½t want to waste either of our time so Iï¿½ll start by asking you some questions about your business and if I think youï¿½re a good fit - Then Iï¿½ll explain what I have to offer and then at the end you can make a decision whether you want to be apart of it or not. Sounds good?


So Lucy {Client Name}, you obviously took the time out of your day to have this meeting for a reason. Whatï¿½s that reason?


{Expect Bullshit Answer}


Okay, so could you show me what your current digital marketing strategy looks like? Just to give me some context


Whatï¿½s the sales process from stranger to you?


Are you happy with your current system of customer acquisition


Look, from audit that I did prior to this meeting - It appears as though youï¿½re leaving a lot of money on the table, but Iï¿½m not here to waste either of our time so I think itï¿½s best of we get to the real number. Would you agree?


How much revenue did your business do last month?


Are you happy with that amount? Were would you like that to be in 12 months?


Great, so youï¿½re at ï¿½xï¿½ per month and you want to get to ï¿½xï¿½ per month - So that space in between is a change that need to be made in your business. Without that change, youï¿½re going to stay exactly where you are.


Lucy - Now, before we continue - Whatï¿½s the motivation behind getting to ï¿½xï¿½?


How would things be different for your business if you got to ï¿½xï¿½?


How would getting to ï¿½xï¿½ effect your employees or your family?


So Lucy, you really have two options at this point. You can either continue to do what youï¿½re doing and stay at ï¿½xï¿½ or you can make a change and get to ï¿½xï¿½. I think youï¿½re a good fit for what I do, so would you like to hear about how I could help you?
